chief_complaint: A 38-year-old Greek male developed severe symptoms, including hematological stress, after his first and only mRNA SARS-CoV-2 vaccination.
history: The patient has a beta-zero thalassemia trait and a history of regular taurine intake (500 mg/day) for years, which helped control lactate and ferritin levels. He developed lactic acidosis and hyperferritinemia after vaccination, with lactate levels > 4 mmol/ml and ferritin levels > 820 ng/ml. Urine tests showed organic acid and plasma metabolite levels indicative of mitochondrial dysfunction.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Lactate levels > 4 mmol/ml, ferritin levels > 820 ng/ml, and urine tests showing organic acid and plasma metabolite levels indicative of mitochondrial dysfunction.
correct_diagnosis: Lactic acidosis and hyperferritinemia after mRNA SARS-CoV-2 vaccination in a patient with beta-zero thalassemia trait
differentials: Mitochondrial myopathy, encephalopathy, lactic acidosis, and stroke-like episodes (MELAS)

correct_ranking: Lactic acidosis and hyperferritinemia after mRNA SARS-CoV-2 vaccination in a patient with beta-zero thalassemia trait
